children.text: Purpose.
The 3-panel editor models a browser-native IDE designed specifically for structured lessons and classroom environments.
Authoring layer.
Markdown-based instructions render alongside an Ace-powered code editor, keeping content and execution tightly coupled while preserving clarity for students.
Execution layer.
A centralized execution router determines how user code runs based on context.
Web projects execute inside a sandboxed
iframe
, preventing cross-context leakage.
Python exercises run in-browser using
Skulpt
, eliminating server dependency while preserving safety.
Terminal output is surfaced directly in the interface, ensuring immediate feedback without breaking user flow.
Persistence layer.
Projects are explicitly saved using a keyboard shortcut and written to AWS S3, enabling consistent cross-device access for both teachers and students.
The architecture balances IDE-level capability with classroom constraints by isolating execution while remaining entirely browser-native.
Purpose.
The organization and license model separates user identity from institutional access control, enabling clean multi-tenant scalability.
Identity and ownership.
Users exist independently of organizations and can maintain personal projects regardless of institutional affiliation.
License enforcement.
Each organization is governed by a centralized license that determines classroom availability through an access gate.
A
valid license
enables full classroom functionality.
An
expired license
transitions projects into read-only mode without deleting or restricting visibility.
This model preserves student work while maintaining strict access governance across organizations.
Separating identity, ownership, and licensing created a scalable foundation for supporting multiple schools without entangling user data with subscription state.

children.text: Purpose.
This flow describes how users move from classroom context into active project work within the platform.
Role-aware entry.
Both teachers and students begin on a unified Classrooms page, but are routed to role-specific class lists before converging at a shared Classroom Dashboard.
Project resolution.
When a lesson is selected, a deterministic project resolver decides how work should be handled.
If prior work exists, the system opens the existing project state.
If no work exists, a new project is cloned from the lesson template and initialized with grading metadata.
This resolver ensured a predictable and low-friction transition from instruction to execution, regardless of user role.

children.text: Content architecture strategy.
The curriculum model is designed around compositional hierarchy and controlled reuse, allowing instructional content to evolve without destabilizing active classrooms.
Hierarchical composition.
Organizations own curriculum dashboards, which organize courses into units and lessons. Each level represents a structural boundary rather than a duplication of content.
Template indirection layer.
Lessons reference stable lesson templates instead of embedding executable content directly. This indirection prevents structural mutations from cascading into live classroom environments.
Runtime classroom boundary.
Classrooms consume curriculum artifacts without owning them, ensuring instructional updates remain centrally managed while student project state remains isolated.
Extensibility model.
Additional resources and metadata can be attached at the lesson layer without mutating template structure, enabling safe iteration by curriculum teams.
By separating structural composition from execution context, the platform achieves safe curriculum evolution across multi-organization deployments.

children.text: Architectural intent.
This hackathon prototype demonstrates a state-aware, voice-driven repair assistant built using event-driven cloud architecture principles.
Input abstraction layer.
Technician speech is captured as a natural input modality and converted into structured text through a speech-to-text pipeline, abstracting physical interaction away from the workflow.
Intent resolution pipeline.
Transcribed commands are routed through an NLP processor and dispatched to AWS Lambda functions, where intent classification and contextual state evaluation occur.
State-aware orchestration.
The system maintains repair-step progression within cloud logic, enabling dynamic instruction resolution based on prior actions rather than static command-response mapping.
Instruction synthesis.
Parsed workflow decisions are transformed into structured procedural guidance and returned as audio output, completing the closed feedback loop without requiring manual device interaction.
Although developed within hackathon constraints, the architecture models a scalable, event-driven service layer capable of supporting real-world, hands-free industrial workflows.

children.text: This project models a Raspberry Pi–based greenhouse controller as a
deterministic, state-aware automation engine

. Rather than treating it as a simple GPIO script, the system is structured as a continuous feedback loop that measures environmental conditions and translates them into deliberate actuator decisions.

At runtime, the controller continuously reads telemetry from a DHT11 sensor and evaluates those readings against configuration defined by the active growth mode. Each cycle performs threshold evaluation and mutates relay state only when necessary, maintaining environmental stability without unpredictable side effects.
The architecture separates concerns into clearly defined layers:
Hardware Layer:
Raspberry Pi, DHT11 sensor, and GPIO-driven relays interfacing with physical systems.
Configuration Layer:
Growth modes encapsulate humidity thresholds, light schedules, and ventilation policy as data rather than embedded logic.
Control Engine:
A rule-based evaluation function that interprets environmental input and produces deterministic plug state updates.

By externalizing growth strategies into declarative mode objects, the controller remains generic and extensible. Adding a new cultivation strategy does not require modifying the decision engine—only supplying a new configuration structure.
The system functions as a closed feedback loop: measure → evaluate → actuate → repeat. This mental model keeps the automation predictable, transparent, and resilient over long-running execution.

The result is a stable embedded control system that runs indefinitely until terminated, continuously steering the greenhouse environment toward its ideal operating conditions while remaining explicit in its state transitions and configuration boundaries.

title: Automation System
description: This diagram shows how environmental sensor data flows into a Raspberry Pi based control system, which applies decision logic before actuating physical devices through a relay board. The design prioritizes reliability and autonomy, allowing the system to regulate climate conditions continuously without human intervention.

Controller->>Relays: Toggle required relays
Relays->>Devices: Activate / deactivate devices
end

children.text: This diagram shows the
client-side encryption pipeline
: UI input → mode selection → alphabet version + magic number → per-character rotation → tag append.
Everything runs locally in the browser—no server dependency—so the original plaintext never leaves the device.
Mode resolution:
User input determines whether encryption or decryption logic is executed.
Cipher configuration:
A random magic number and alphabet version (v1–v9) are selected per operation.
Character rotation:
Each character is rotated using position-aware alphabet mapping.
Self-describing output:
Encryption metadata is appended as a tag to enable automatic future decryption.

%% --------------------------------------------------

%% --------------------------------------------------

children.text: This diagram focuses purely on the
decryption path
. The system detects the encryption tag, extracts the embedded metadata, resolves the correct alphabet version, and reverses the rotation per character.
The tag contains just enough information to make decryption deterministic — no stored keys or server lookups required.

}}%%

flowchart TD

Input["Encrypted Text"]
Detect["Detect Encryption Tag"]
Parse["Extract Version + Magic Number"]
Resolve["Resolve Alphabet Set"]
Reverse["Reverse Character Rotation"]
Output["Decrypted Output"]

Input --> Detect
Detect --> Parse
Parse --> Resolve
Resolve --> Reverse
Reverse --> Output

children.text: This sequence diagram illustrates how Enigma
automatically detects and reverses encrypted input
using the metadata embedded inside the encryption tag.
The UI detects the presence of an encryption tag in the pasted input.
The tag parser extracts the alphabet version and magic number.
The version resolver selects the correct alphabet set (v1–v9).
The cipher engine reverses the per-character rotation deterministically.
The decrypted result is rendered immediately with no configuration required.
Decryption is intentionally frictionless — users never need to remember how encryption was performed.

Resolver ->> Engine: Resolve alphabet set
Engine ->> Engine: Reverse rotation per character
Engine ->> Output: Decrypted text
Output -->> User: Display result

title: Domain Model
description: The domain model outlines the hierarchical structure used to manage campaigns in the D20 King project. Storybooks decompose into acts, rooms, encounters, and rewards, enabling complex narratives to remain organized and scalable.

title: GIF Freak – System Flow
description: This diagram shows how user input propagates through the UI to fetch data from the Giphy API and render results dynamically. It represents early hands-on experience with asynchronous requests and reactive interfaces.

title: Stock Memer – Data & Content Flow
description: This diagram illustrates how live stock data and user-generated content move through the application. It highlights the interaction between external APIs, client-side processing, and Firebase persistence.

title: Matching Algorithm Flow
description: This flow represents how survey responses are processed through a compatibility algorithm to generate ranked matches. It reflects a shift toward building purpose-driven systems with real-world impact.
